title: Right operands of shift operators should be integers
description: Right operands of shift operators should be integersAnalyze your codeintentionality-completereliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoNumbers can be shifted with the<<and>>operators, but the right operand of the operation needs to be anintor a type that has animplicit conversiontoint. However, when the left operand isdynamic, the compiler’s type checking is turned off, so you can pass anything to the right of a shift operator and have it compile. And if the argument can’t be implicitly converted tointat runtime, then aRuntimeBinderExceptionwill be raised.dynamic d = 5; var x = d >> 5.4; // Noncompliant x = d << null; // Noncompliant x <<= new object(); // NoncompliantAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitL
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
dynamic d = 5;
var x = d >> 5.4; // Double used as shift amount

int value = 8;
value <<= null; // Null used as shift amount

dynamic val = 10;
val << new object(); // Object used as shift amount

long number = 100L;
result = number >> 2.5f; // Float used as shift amount
compliant:
int value = 8;
value <<= 2; // Integer shift amount

long number = 100L;
result = number >> 3; // Integer shift amount

byte flags = 0x01;
flags <<= 1; // Valid integer operation

uint mask = 0xFF;
mask >>= 4; // Valid unsigned integer operation

conditions: Right operand is not an integer (float, null, object, etc.)

exclusion_conditions: Right operand is integer literal or variable
logic.reasoning: Detects shift operations where the right operand is not an integer type, which can cause RuntimeBinderException with dynamic types or compilation errors. Identifies patterns where floating point numbers, null, objects, or method calls are used as shift amounts instead of integers.
